channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Indonesia says its coast guard drove away Chinese ship that interrupted survey in disputed sea
item.description: JAKARTA, Indonesia (AP) — Indonesian patrol ships drove a Chinese coast guard vessel away from a survey vessel in a disputed area of the South China Sea for the second time in three days, Indonesian authorities said Thursday.
item.dc:creator: EDNA TARIGAN, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Putin presides over BRICS summit seeking to expand Russia's clout
item.description: KAZAN, Russia (AP) — Russian President Vladimir Putin on Thursday presided over a closing session of a summit of the BRICS bloc of developing economies, hailing its role as a counterbalance to the West.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: EPA imposes stricter standards to protect children from exposure to lead paint
item.description: The Biden administration is imposing strict new limits on dust from lead-based paint in older homes and child-care facilities.
item.dc:creator: Matthew Daly, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Beloved Grand Teton grizzly bear No. 399 fatally struck by a vehicle in Wyoming
item.description: Grand Teton National Park’s famous grizzly bear No. 399, known by wildlife photographers around the world, died after being struck by a vehicle Tuesday in Wyoming.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Suspect in shootings of Democratic office in Arizona had over 200 guns in his home, officials find
item.description: TEMPE, Ariz. (AP) — An Arizona prosecutor said the man arrested in the shooting of a Democratic National Committee office in suburban Phoenix had more than 200 guns and over 250,000 rounds of ammunition in his home, leading law enforcement to believe he may have been planning a mass casualty event.
item.dc:creator: ANITA SNOW and WALTER BERRY, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: DNA links suspected serial killer to 19-year-old's cold case murder in Illinois
item.description: Authorities have linked a suspected serial killer to the kidnapping and murder of a 19-year-old Illinois woman, more than 45 years after her death.
item.dc:creator: Eli Ong, KRON4

item.title: DNA helps Illinois police make breakthrough in 1970s cold case murder of 19-year-old
item.description: An Illinois police department announced it cracked a cold case homicide from the 1970s through DNA testing. It said 19-year-old Kathy Halle was murdered by serial killer Bruce Lindahl.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Georgia, candidates target of ‘sustained’ cyberattacks: Reports
item.description: CNN says Georgia fended off the likely overseas attack, while Microsoft says China, Russia and Iran are targeting the U.S. election process.
item.dc:creator: Rich Johnson, NewsNation

item.title: Ron Ely, TV's 'Tarzan' in the 1960s, dies at 86
item.description: Ron Ely, the tall, musclebound actor who played the title character in the 1960s NBC series “Tarzan,” has died at age 86.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Florida mailman stabs lawncare worker over parking: deputies
item.description: The Hillsborough County Sheriff's Office has released more information about the events that may have led up to a deadly stabbing Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Sierra Rains, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Well-known pilot Chuck Coleman killed in air show crash in Las Cruces
item.description: The pilot who was killed in a crash at a Las Cruces air show over the weekend has been identified as Charles Thomas "Chuck" Coleman of California, the City of Las Cruces said in an update issued Monday afternoon, Oct. 21.
item.dc:creator: Dave Burge, Fox 5

item.title: A hurricane's historic destruction hasn't stopped North Carolina election workers and voters
item.description: HOT SPRINGS, N.C. (AP) — About three weeks after Hurricane Helene pummeled western North Carolina, the Hot Springs Community Center was still caked in mud. A paper sign hung on the door warning visitors to use “extreme caution” until the building could be inspected.
item.dc:creator: MAKIYA SEMINERA, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Despite E. coli outbreak, McDonald's says customers should feel confident eating at chain
item.description: McDonald’s U.S. President Joe Erlinger said in an interview on the “Today” show that the company has removed the Quarter Pounder from its menu in the 10 states where dozens of people were sickened, as well as in several other states.
item.dc:creator: Dee-Ann Durbin, KRON4

item.title: Over 7,100 bottles of prescription antidepressants recalled over potentially cancer-causing substance
item.description: A pharmaceutical company issued a recall this month for thousands of bottles of duloxetine, a medicine often prescribed to treat depression.
item.dc:creator: Matthew Euzarraga, Fox 5

item.title: Yelp turns off comments on the McDonald's that Trump worked at after fake reviews
item.description: Yelp recently paused reviews on a McDonald's in Pennsylvania after former President Trump served pre-screened customers at the location Sunday.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, ABC 4

item.title: Surfer dies after being struck, impaled by swordfish
item.description: A longtime surfer and travel guide died after being impaled by a swordfish off the coast of Indonesia.
item.dc:creator: Tony Kurzweil, ABC 4

item.title: LeBron and Bronny James make history as the NBA's first father-son duo to play together
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — LeBron James gave his 20-year-old son a pep talk before they rose from the Lakers bench. Amid rising cheers, they walked together to the scorer's table — and then they stepped straight into basketball history.
item.dc:creator: GREG BEACHAM, Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: Dodgers phenom Fernando Valenzuela dies at 63
item.description: Fernando Valenzuela, the Mexican-born phenom for the Los Angeles Dodgers who inspired “Fernandomania” while winning the NL Cy Young Award and Rookie of the Year in 1981, has died.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Dodgers legend Fernando Valenzuela dies at 63
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Fernando Valenzuela, the Mexican-born phenom for the Los Angeles Dodgers who inspired “Fernandomania” while winning the NL Cy Young Award and Rookie of the Year in 1981, has died. He was 63.
item.dc:creator: BETH HARRIS, Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: Court upholds freedom for woman whose conviction was overturned after 43 years behind bars
item.description: An appellate court in Missouri ruled Tuesday that a lower court was right when it decided to overturn the murder conviction of a woman who spent 43 years behind bars for a killing that her attorneys argue was committed by a discredited police officer.
item.dc:creator: HEATHER HOLLINGSWORTH, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Missouri woman finds trunk full of stolen campaign signs with help of Apple AirTag
item.description: "I expected to find the AirTag, but not 59 signs. It was kind of like finding a dead body," the Missouri woman said.
item.dc:creator: Parker Padgett, WFLA, NBC

item.title: MLB wants the Tampa Bay Rays to play in the area if hurricane-damaged Tropicana Field is unfit
item.description: ST. PETERSBURG, Fla. (AP) — Major League Baseball hopes to find the Tampa Bay Rays a temporary home near their fanbase at least for the start of the 2025 season if the damage to Tropicana Field from Hurricane Milton cannot be repaired in time. The translucent fiberglass dome at the ballpark in St.
item.dc:creator: CURT ANDERSON, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Los Angeles Times won't endorse Harris for president — or Trump
item.description: When Southern California residents go to fill out their ballots in the Nov. 5 election, they might rely on the Los Angeles Times' endorsements for guidance. But if you're looking for a recommendation for president from the Times, you're out of luck. Dr.
item.dc:creator: Cameron Kiszla, KTLA 5

item.title: 19-year-old woman found dead in oven in Canada Walmart
item.description: A Canadian Walmart store was closed for a third day Tuesday as police investigated the death of a woman whose body was found inside an oven.
item.dc:creator: Wil Day, ABC 4

item.title: Insurance company refuses to pay 82-year-old Las Vegas woman's claim after her home burned down
item.description: Two weeks before the fire, the insurance company canceled the woman's policy, accusing her of running a motorcycle repair shop out of her home — something the 82-year-old woman denies.
item.dc:creator: Kyle J. Paine, KRON4

item.title: Mike Jeffries, former Abercrombie & Fitch CEO, arrested on federal sex trafficking charges
item.description: Former Abercrombie & Fitch CEO Michael Jeffries and two other men have been arrested on sex trafficking and interstate prostitution charges, a spokesperson for federal prosecutors said Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Trump makes more debunked claims about FEMA as he visits storm damaged North Carolina
item.description: Trump was asked whether it was helpful to criticize hurricane relief workers after the Federal Emergency Management Agency recently paused work in the area because of reports they could be targeted by militia.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Judges reject GOP overseas ballot challenges in Michigan, North Carolina
item.description: State judges in Michigan and North Carolina on Monday rejected the Republican National Committee’s (RNC) separate challenges to certain overseas ballots in the key swing states.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, Fox 5

item.title: New campus protest rules spur an outcry from college faculty
item.description: Dissent is thriving this fall at American colleges, and not just among student activists. With student protests limited by new restrictions, faculty have taken up the cause. To faculty, new protest rules threaten freedom of speech — and the freedom to think, both central to university life.
item.dc:creator: ANNIE MA, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Texas man whose execution was halted doesn't appear at state Capitol after subpoena
item.description: AUSTIN, Texas (AP) — A Texas man whose execution was halted and then called to testify at the state Capitol did not show up Monday over growing pushback from state leaders who have blasted lawmakers' last-ditch tactics to subpoena Robert Roberson and spare his life for now.
item.dc:creator: NADIA LATHAN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Judge hears arguments to block Louisiana's Ten Commandments displays in schools
item.description: BATON ROUGE, La. (AP) — A federal judge heard arguments at a hearing Monday on whether he should temporarily block a new Louisiana law that requires the Ten Commandments to be displayed in every public school classroom by Jan. 1.
item.dc:creator: SARA CLINE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: What is pink cocaine?: The drug may be connected to Liam Payne's death
item.description: A partial autopsy conducted on former One Direction singer Liam Payne found multiple substances in his system when he fell to his death from the third-floor balcony of a hotel room in Argentina last week, according to a new report from ABC News.
item.dc:creator: Derick Fox, ABC 4

item.title: Harris raises more than $220 million in September, outraising Trump 3-to-1
item.description: Vice President Kamala Harris’s campaign raised nearly $222 million in September, raising more than three times as much as former President Donald Trump’s campaign.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, Fox 5

item.title: Over 1 million voters cast early ballots in North Carolina
item.description: Nearly 1.01 million North Carolinians have already voted in the November general election, state officials reported on Sunday.
item.dc:creator: Sophia Vento, Fox 5

item.title: Trump tells child there will be no cows under Harris
item.description: Former President Donald Trump joined Fox and Friends on Friday for an in-person interview where he told a 6-year-old child that the U.S. “won’t have any cows” if Vice President Kamala Harris is elected.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, WGN 9
